briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: The candidate field. Turns your edges, interests, and constraints plus live market signals into 10-15 scored venture candidates — divergence before any deep dive.
briefs.title: Opportunity Scan

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: One niche, fully mapped — the players, buyers, channels, watering holes, jargon, money flows, and unwritten rules — before you decide to enter it.
briefs.title: Niche Map

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: Proof people actually hurt — verbatim complaints mined from reviews and forums, search and hiring signals, and what they already pay to make the pain stop.
briefs.title: Pain & Demand Mining

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: Everyone already fighting for this money — features, pricing, positioning, and traction compared, their customers' complaints mined, and the gaps nobody covers.
briefs.title: Competitor Teardown

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: Bottom-up sizing with the arithmetic shown, growth read from primary signals, and the why-now case argued both ways — no analyst-deck TAM theater.
briefs.title: Market Size & Timing

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: How to enter and be understood — the beachhead customer, the against-what frame, the category call, and message options tested against real competitor language.
briefs.title: Positioning & Wedge

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: Why this survives contact with incumbents and arithmetic — monetization options, napkin unit economics, defensibility paths, and the kill risks named out loud.
briefs.title: Moat & Model Check

briefs.question: is it worth building?
briefs.tagline: The decider. Reads every venture report at this root, scores the candidate against bars set before scoring, and rules go, pivot, or kill — reasoning shown.
briefs.title: Venture Verdict

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: The flagship. Understand the whole product, then surface prioritized opportunities across UI, UX, retention, engagement, community, and beauty.
briefs.title: Product Improvement Discovery

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: How user signal re-enters the product — corrections captured or evaporating, thumbs nobody reads, and the complaint-to-fix path.
briefs.title: Feedback Loop Audit

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: Every message the product sends out of the app — transactional email, push, in-app — for whether it is timely, wanted, clear, and controllable.
briefs.title: Notification & Email Audit

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: The product's own search — whether users can find what they are looking for: relevance, recall, forgiveness, and what happens on zero results.
briefs.title: In-App Search Audit

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: The whole arc of an account beyond signup — settings, export, plan changes, and deletion — and whether the product treats users with dignity, even leaving.
briefs.title: Account Lifecycle Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Trace latent bugs, unhandled edge cases, and race conditions; rank by user pain with repro paths and fix sketches.
briefs.title: Bug Hunt

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Map what is tested against what is riskiest, and produce a test-writing plan that buys the most safety per hour.
briefs.title: Test Gap Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Find swallowed errors, missing logs, and monitoring blind spots — everywhere the system can break without anyone knowing.
briefs.title: Silent Failure Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: The bugs that only appear under load — races, shared mutable state, and ordering assumptions that hold on a quiet laptop and break in production.
briefs.title: Concurrency & Race-Condition Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Where the type system has been defeated or never engaged — the casts, anys, and untyped boundaries where the compiler stops protecting you.
briefs.title: Type-Safety Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Whether the tests actually protect the code — asserting meaningful behavior, or merely executing lines and passing no matter what.
briefs.title: Test-Quality Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: The tests that fail intermittently — the ones eroding trust in the suite until a red build stops meaning anything.
briefs.title: Flaky-Test Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Count and time the suite by layer, draw the shape it actually makes, and compute confidence-per-second — the number that says which tests earn their runtime.
briefs.title: Test-Pyramid Balance

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: The broad latency pass — wasted renders, main-thread stalls, startup waterfalls — ranked by what the user actually feels on common paths.
briefs.title: Performance Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Simulate 10x users, data, and traffic on paper — find what breaks first, at what threshold, and the cheapest mitigation.
briefs.title: 10x Stress Review

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Where the seconds go. Decomposes p50 and p95 of the runs users feel into stages — queue, retrieval, first token, tools — and names the stage worth attacking.
briefs.title: Latency Budget Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: The database access patterns that fall over as data and traffic grow — N+1s, missing indexes, unbounded reads, and lock contention.
briefs.title: Query Performance & N+1 Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: What the browser actually downloads — oversized bundles, unsplit code, heavy dependencies, and unoptimized assets that slow first paint.
briefs.title: Bundle & Asset Weight Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Every cache from CDN headers to that module-level dict — keys, TTLs, who invalidates what — the stale read, the stampede, and the key that leaks between users.
briefs.title: Caching Strategy Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: A defensive review of your own codebase — auth gaps, injection surfaces, exposed secrets, and data leaks, ranked by exploitability.
briefs.title: Security & Privacy Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Vulnerable, abandoned, oversized, or duplicated packages — the full health check on every dependency this project stands on, with removal candidates named.
briefs.title: Dependency Health Check

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Keyboard navigation, contrast, labels, and screen-reader flow — audited end-to-end along the core user journey.
briefs.title: Accessibility Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Hardcoded strings, locale-blind number and date formatting, and layouts that break in translation — every gap between you and a non-default-locale user.
briefs.title: Localization Readiness

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Every dependency's license, the ones incompatible with how you ship, the attribution you owe, and the copyleft reaching into your own source.
briefs.title: License & Compliance

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Every hardcoded key, token, and password the repo carries — in source, config, and git history — plus how they rotate and who can read them.
briefs.title: Secrets & Credential Hygiene

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Who can do what — whether every sensitive action and record checks the caller's permission, not just that they are logged in.
briefs.title: Access-Control & Authorization Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Trace untrusted input to dangerous sinks — where attacker-controlled data becomes a query, a command, markup, a request, or a file path.
briefs.title: Input & Injection Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: An attacker's-eye map — the assets worth stealing, the surfaces that reach them, and the abuse cases the product does not yet defend.
briefs.title: Threat Model & Abuse Cases

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Run the outdated command and read the lockfile's birthdays — majors behind, EOL dates, the two packages pinning everything — and the forced jump the drift is scheduling.
briefs.title: Dependency Currency Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Walk the product the way assistive-tech users do — keyboard only, then with a screen reader — and find every place that path breaks.
briefs.title: Keyboard & Screen-Reader Flow

briefs.question: does it respect the user?
briefs.tagline: Whether the product collects consent honestly and honors it — the cookies, trackers, and data collection that fire before or despite the user's choice.
briefs.title: Consent & Cookie Audit

briefs.question: does it respect the user?
briefs.tagline: Whether the product can honor the rights users have over their data — access, export, correction, and deletion — operationally, not just in the privacy policy.
briefs.title: Data-Subject-Rights Readiness

briefs.question: does it respect the user?
briefs.tagline: How the product protects data with encryption — in transit, at rest, and at the field level — and whether the keys that unlock it are managed safely.
briefs.title: Encryption & Key Management

briefs.question: does it respect the user?
briefs.tagline: Whether the system keeps a trustworthy record of who did what — the audit trail that compliance, incident response, and forensics all depend on.
briefs.title: Audit-Trail Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Reconstruct the signup → activation → habit funnel from code, count the steps, and rank every drop-off point.
briefs.title: Funnel Friction Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Research real rivals on the web, then split the gaps: table stakes you must match vs differentiation bets only you can make.
briefs.title: Competitive Gap Scan

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Find the premium-worthy features, the natural upgrade moments, and the billing friction between users and paying you.
briefs.title: Monetization Map

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Where an LLM feature genuinely earns its place — automation, drafting, summarization — and where it would be a gimmick.
briefs.title: AI Opportunity Scan

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Titles, metadata, structured data, crawlability, and social unfurls — every place a search engine or a shared link fails to find, index, or render your pages.
briefs.title: SEO & Discoverability

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Audit every conversion surface — hero, pricing, signup, checkout — against eCommerce best practices, and rank the highest-leverage lifts for a first-time visitor.
briefs.title: Conversion Rate Optimization

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: The return trip: the saved state, resurfaced value, and well-timed nudges that earn a second visit — and the churn cliffs that quietly lose it.
briefs.title: Retention & Lifecycle Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Testimonials, logos, counts, reviews, and guarantees — the evidence that turns a skeptical stranger into a believer, and the credibility gaps where doubt wins.
briefs.title: Social Proof & Credibility

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: The first session: empty states, a guided first action, and the fastest path to a first win that leaves a newcomer inspired to build the next thing.
briefs.title: Activation & First-Win Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Every form in the product — whether it asks the least, validates kindly, and recovers gracefully, or leaks users at the moment they try to give you something.
briefs.title: Forms & Validation Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: The path from intent to paid — whether checkout converts or leaks, and whether the payment integration survives declines, retries, and webhook redelivery.
briefs.title: Checkout & Payment Flow Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Duplication, tangles, and dead patterns — mapped as interest paid vs principal owed, with a refactor sequence that pays for itself.
briefs.title: Tech Debt Map

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Walk clone → install → run → change → PR like a brand-new developer and log every stumble on the way to a first contribution.
briefs.title: New-Dev Onboarding Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Census the drifted colors, spacing, and duplicate components; propose the token set and migration order to unify them.
briefs.title: Design System Consolidation

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Can a coding agent work here? Entry files, one-command checks, discoverable conventions, and a mechanical definition of done — audited by attempting the work.
briefs.title: Agent Readiness Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Read git history for the human risks — files only one person understands, critical paths with a truck factor of one, and the knowledge that leaves when they do.
briefs.title: Ownership & Bus Factor

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: The edit to build to test to see-it-work cycle, timed — and the daily friction that taxes every change the team makes.
briefs.title: Inner-Loop Speed Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: How hard it is to find out why something broke — whether the system helps a developer localize a failure or forces guesswork.
briefs.title: Debuggability Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: CI as a feedback loop — how fast, trustworthy, and clear the signal is between pushing code and knowing it is safe to merge.
briefs.title: CI Feedback-Loop Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Whether the consequential technical decisions are written down — or whether the why lives only in people's heads and git archaeology.
briefs.title: Decision-Record Audit

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: The webhooks this product sends and receives — delivery guarantees, retries, signing, and whether a consumer can actually build on them reliably.
briefs.title: Webhook Design Audit

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: The client libraries developers use to call this product — how fast they reach a first successful call, and how the SDK behaves when things go wrong.
briefs.title: SDK Ergonomics Audit

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: How the API evolves without breaking the people who built on it — the versioning scheme, breaking-change discipline, and how deprecations are announced and retired.
briefs.title: API Versioning & Deprecation

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: The API's rate limits and quotas as a product surface — whether they protect the system while staying fair, predictable, and workable for real consumers.
briefs.title: Rate-Limit & Quota Design

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: The first hour a developer spends with this API — from landing on the docs to a working integration — and where they stall, guess, or give up.
briefs.title: Developer Portal & Onboarding

briefs.question: will developers adopt it?
briefs.tagline: Read the package the way a stranger's bundler does — every export weighed, typed, and judged: the accidental API, the semver debt, and what the next major must break.
briefs.title: Library & Package API Design

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Follow the docs literally to find where they lie, then rank the gaps by who gets blocked — and which wrong docs to delete.
briefs.title: Documentation Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Extract every user-facing string; hunt jargon, vague CTAs, and unhelpful errors; rewrite the worst ten before/after.
briefs.title: Copy & Voice Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Naming, error shapes, status codes, and versioning — find the inconsistencies and sequence fixes from non-breaking to breaking.
briefs.title: API Contract Review

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Can a newcomer explain back what this is, who it's for, and how it works after one screen — or does the curse of knowledge leave them guessing?
briefs.title: Comprehension Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Every message the product shows when something goes wrong — whether it says what happened, why, and what to do next, or leaves the user stuck.
briefs.title: Error-Message Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Run the binary like a stranger — help screens, wrong flags, bad input, exit codes, --json — an annotated transcript of where the CLI teaches and where it bites.
briefs.title: CLI Tool UX Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Where the eye actually goes versus where it should — focal points, size and weight and contrast doing real work, and the screens where everything shouts at once.
briefs.title: Visual Hierarchy Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The type scale, or the lack of one — sizes, weights, line lengths and heights, font loading, and every place text is asked to do a job at the wrong size.
briefs.title: Typography Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The palette as it actually ships — every hex in the codebase, contrast ratios computed, semantic drift named, and whether dark mode is a theme or an apology.
briefs.title: Color & Contrast Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The spacing scale nobody wrote down — margins and paddings inventoried, grid discipline checked, alignment breaks found, and density judged screen by screen.
briefs.title: Spacing & Layout Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The state matrix every element owes you — hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error — plus motion that means something versus transitions that just jiggle.
briefs.title: Interaction States & Motion Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Does this look like one thing made on purpose — logo usage, icon language, imagery, radius and shadow DNA — audited against what the brand claims to be.
briefs.title: Brand Coherence Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Are the benefits and the how-it-works shown — product shots, demos, diagrams, before-and-afters — or buried in paragraphs a visitor has to take on faith?
briefs.title: Show, Don't Tell

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The product on a phone — whether layouts, touch targets, and performance hold up on small screens and real devices, or quietly assume a desktop.
briefs.title: Mobile & Responsive Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Whether the design system's tokens are actually used — or whether hardcoded colors, spacing, and type have crept back in and drifted the UI out of sync.
briefs.title: Design-Token Adoption Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The three questions every screen owes a user — where am I, where can I go, how do I get back — plus the nav that hides, the dead ends, and the jargon labels.
briefs.title: Navigation & Wayfinding Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Menus, dropdowns, context menus, and command palettes — do they reveal what is inside, work by keyboard and thumb, and still find things as the list grows?
briefs.title: Menu & Command Surface Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Every chart, graph, and metric put to the truth test — right form, honest axes, labeled and legible, accessible beyond color, and numbers that match their source.
briefs.title: Data Visualization Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: Whether a dashboard answers its question in one glance — the metric that matters winning the top-left, signal over decoration, and density chosen, not accidental.
briefs.title: Dashboard & Density Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The screens users meet with nothing yet — first run, no results, cleared, deleted, error-empty — and whether each teaches the next step or just shows a blank and a shrug.
briefs.title: Empty & Zero-Data States Audit

briefs.question: is it beautiful?
briefs.tagline: The icon set as it actually ships — one visual language or a ransom note, every glyph readable and labeled, meanings consistent, and weight kept in check.
briefs.title: Iconography & Visual Language Audit

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Missing indexes, absent constraints, JSON blobs hiding structure — with a migration plan sequenced safest-first.
briefs.title: Schema Review

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Which user behaviors are invisible today — and the tracking plan that makes the five questions that matter answerable.
briefs.title: Analytics Coverage Audit

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Trace where personal data enters, spreads, and lingers — then run the delete-request test: could you honor one today?
briefs.title: PII & Retention Map

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Mine the git history for churn, bug magnets, coupled files, and bus-factor silos — predict where the next bug lands.
briefs.title: Git Hotspot Mining

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Schema and data migrations under real traffic — table locks, reversibility, timed-out backfills, and the deploy ordering that turns a change into an outage.
briefs.title: Migration Safety

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Whether the data can be trusted — the constraints and checks that prevent the corrupt, orphaned, and contradictory rows that quietly accumulate.
briefs.title: Data Integrity Audit

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Whether the numbers the product reports mean the same thing everywhere — so active user, revenue, and signup are not defined three different ways.
briefs.title: Metric Definition Consistency

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Diff what producers write against what consumers assume — schema drift, ordering bets, missing idempotency keys — and find the message that breaks when a deploy staggers.
briefs.title: Event & Message Contracts

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Run every job twice on paper and yesterday once more — duplicates, half-written partitions, now() in a backfill — and a ledger of who notices stale data.
briefs.title: Data Pipeline Quality

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Map merged-PR to production; every manual step is a finding. Includes the rollback-readiness verdict.
briefs.title: Release Pipeline Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Expensive queries, chatty API loops, unbounded storage, oversized resources — savings ranked by effort.
briefs.title: Cost Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: The 3am table — for each failure scenario: how you'd detect it, how you'd respond, and the gap in between.
briefs.title: Incident Readiness Review

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Every knob, var, and magic value — where config sprawls, where environments drift, what explodes at boot versus at 3am, and which secrets hide in plain sight.
briefs.title: Config & Environment Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: The positive side of observability — whether metrics, traces, and SLOs let you answer any question about the running system, not just whether it crashed.
briefs.title: Telemetry & SLOs

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Whether this system could actually come back from data loss — whether backups exist, cover what matters, and have ever been proven to restore.
briefs.title: Backup & Recovery Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Whether a bad change can be undone fast — whether the system ships behind flags, rolls back cleanly, and has kill switches for its riskiest paths.
briefs.title: Feature-Flag & Rollback Readiness

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: How tightly the code is bound to specific vendors and clouds — and what it would cost to leave if pricing, terms, or reliability forced the issue.
briefs.title: Vendor Lock-In Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Read the Terraform, K8s, and Dockerfiles the way the machine will — :latest tags, plaintext secrets, copy-pasted stanzas — and map the blast radius of one bad apply.
briefs.title: Infra-as-Code Audit

briefs.question: will it stay up?
briefs.tagline: What happens when a dependency is slow or down — whether the product degrades gracefully with timeouts, fallbacks, and breakers, or cascades into a full outage.
briefs.title: Graceful Degradation Audit

briefs.question: will it stay up?
briefs.tagline: Kill every dependency on paper — trace the actual catch, timeout, and fallback code, or its absence — and script the game day before production improvises one.
briefs.title: Failure-Injection Readiness

briefs.question: will it stay up?
briefs.tagline: Every ceiling the config actually sets — pools, workers, queue bounds — read into a headroom ledger naming what saturates first, and at what multiple of today.
briefs.title: Capacity & Scalability Audit

briefs.question: will it stay up?
briefs.tagline: Whether the system defends itself against traffic that is not a well-behaved user — bots, scrapers, floods, and expensive requests that can exhaust it.
briefs.title: Abuse & Overload Protection

briefs.question: what should go?
briefs.tagline: Dead code, unreachable routes, stale flags, unused deps — a deletion list grouped by confidence. Git makes it reversible; bias to delete.
briefs.title: Prune Audit

briefs.question: what should go?
briefs.tagline: Abstractions with one implementation, pass-through layers, config nobody changes — ranked by clarity gained per risk.
briefs.title: Simplification Pass

briefs.question: do the reports add up?
briefs.tagline: The capstone. Reads every audit report in the repo, dedupes overlapping findings, and merges them into one sequenced plan.
briefs.title: Roadmap Synthesis

briefs.question: do the reports add up?
briefs.tagline: A fast weekly vitals pass — tests, build, deps, TODOs — diffed against the last run so drift shows up as trend arrows.
briefs.title: Recurring Health Check

briefs.question: does anything change?
briefs.tagline: The router. Fifteen minutes of recon that names which briefs this repo actually needs, in what order, and which families it can skip with a clear conscience.
briefs.title: Audit Triage

briefs.question: does anything change?
briefs.tagline: Turns your root reports into commits. Asks the scope — all, high-priority, or its pick — then builds them in dependency order, one verified commit each.
briefs.title: The Fixer

briefs.question: does anything change?
briefs.tagline: Point it at a diff, branch, or PR: blast radius, the tests that should exist, the migration and rollback story, and a go / no-go with the risks ranked.
briefs.title: Change Risk Review

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Every system prompt in the repo: contradictions, dead instructions, bloat, and injection surface — with a consolidation plan.
briefs.title: Prompt & Instruction Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Your tools are an API whose consumer is a model — audit names, descriptions, error contracts, and guards on destructive actions.
briefs.title: Tool Design Review

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: How runs end — iteration caps, stop conditions, stuck-state detection, and the true cost of one pathological run.
briefs.title: Loop & Termination Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Reconstruct what actually enters the model's window each step — stale payloads, token hogs, retrieval junk, and buried instructions.
briefs.title: Context Engineering Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: The test-gap audit for nondeterminism — golden sets, regression evals, judge quality, and the drift between eval and production.
briefs.title: Eval Coverage Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: A defensive review of your agent's blast radius — injection paths, unvalidated outputs, over-broad permissions, ungated irreversible actions.
briefs.title: Guardrails Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Model-to-task fit across every call site — where cheaper and faster would do, where quality is starved, and what happens when a provider blinks.
briefs.title: Model Strategy Review

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Can you see why a run did what it did — per-step traces, failure taxonomy, and replaying yesterday's bad run today.
briefs.title: Trace & Replay Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Cost per completed task, not per call — retry waste, context bloat priced out, cache leverage, and the margin math.
briefs.title: Token Economics Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: What the agent remembers between runs — where memories get written, when they go stale, how a wrong fact gets in, and whether anything can ever be forgotten.
briefs.title: Memory & State Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: The RAG pipeline end to end — chunking that respects meaning, embeddings that don't drift, a relevance floor, and evals that catch regressions before users do.
briefs.title: Retrieval Quality Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Is the org chart of agents earning its overhead — role clarity, orchestration fit, the communication tax, and what one agent with better tools would do instead.
briefs.title: Multi-Agent Topology Review

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: Crash the automation at step 3 of 7 on paper — resumability, idempotency, duplicate side effects, poison inputs, dead letters.
briefs.title: Workflow Resilience Audit

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: LLM calls that should be plain code, rigid code that should be judgment — redraw the line between deterministic and model-driven.
briefs.title: Determinism Boundary Audit

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: Every agent-to-human and agent-to-agent baton pass — context dropped, work duplicated, escalations landing where nobody looks.
briefs.title: Handoff Audit

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: What waiting on an agent feels like — progress honesty, interruptibility, showing the work, and salvaging partial results.
briefs.title: Agent Experience Audit

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: Approval gates audited for rubber-stamp fatigue and silent damage — who reviews what, when, with enough context to actually judge.
briefs.title: Human-in-the-Loop Placement

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: Confidence theater vs honest hedging — uncertainty channels, citation integrity, and what the product does after being wrong.
briefs.title: Trust Calibration Audit

briefs.question: is the AI responsible?
briefs.tagline: Find the code that ranks, scores, or filters people, list the exact fields it consumes, and check whether any eval ever sliced results by group — suspicions labeled.
briefs.title: Bias & Fairness Audit

briefs.question: is the AI responsible?
briefs.tagline: Whether the product's AI outputs are grounded in real sources or confidently invented — and whether it cites, hedges, and refuses when it should.
briefs.title: Hallucination & Grounding Audit

briefs.question: is the AI responsible?
briefs.tagline: Attack the product's own AI like an adversary — make it ignore instructions, leak secrets, or misuse tools — and find where untrusted input can hijack it.
briefs.title: Prompt-Injection Red-Team

briefs.question: is the AI responsible?
briefs.tagline: Diff what the UI says about the AI against what the code does — disclosure strings quoted, dropped confidence traced, retention claims checked against real payloads.
briefs.title: Model Transparency Audit

briefs.question: is the AI responsible?
briefs.tagline: Whether the data used to train, fine-tune, or retrieve is licensed, clean, and free of the PII and contamination that create legal and quality risk.
briefs.title: Training-Data Provenance

playbooks.desc: First contact with an unfamiliar codebase: what is it, is it broken, is it safe, can I work on it.
playbooks.name: Day-1 New Repo
playbooks.tagline: The four questions to ask any repo you just cloned.

playbooks.desc: The night-before checks: safe, accessible, deployable, ready for a bad day.
playbooks.name: Pre-Launch
playbooks.tagline: Everything you should check before you press deploy.

playbooks.desc: Make it smaller and clearer: delete, simplify, then map what debt remains.
playbooks.name: Spring Cleaning
playbooks.tagline: Cut the dead weight, then see what's really left.

playbooks.desc: Ten minutes, trend arrows. Re-run every week.
playbooks.name: Weekly Vitals
playbooks.tagline: A ten-minute weekly health read with trend arrows.

playbooks.desc: First contact with an agent codebase: read its mind, its hands, its loops, its traces.
playbooks.name: Agent Day-1
playbooks.tagline: Understand an AI-agent codebase before you touch it.

playbooks.desc: Before an agent touches real users: guarded, evaluated, supervised, affordable.
playbooks.name: Agent Ship-Check
playbooks.tagline: Is your agent safe to put in front of real users?

playbooks.desc: Close the loop: recon names the audits this repo needs, you run your picks, then the Fixer turns their findings into verified commits.
playbooks.name: Triage & Fix
playbooks.tagline: From “what's wrong?” to shipped fixes, one loop.

playbooks.desc: For RAG-shaped agents: how context gets built, whether retrieval returns the right things, and the evals that keep it that way.
playbooks.name: Retrieval Tune-Up
playbooks.tagline: Make your RAG agent retrieve the right things.

playbooks.desc: The visual overhaul: hierarchy first, then layout, type, and color — and the Fixer turns the findings into commits.
playbooks.name: Face-Lift
playbooks.tagline: A guided visual overhaul that ends in real commits.

playbooks.desc: Can a user find their way around? Map the navigation and menus, check what wins the eye on every screen, and confirm it all still works on a phone.
playbooks.name: Wayfinding
playbooks.tagline: Can users find their way around — on desktop and on a phone?

playbooks.desc: The visualization pass: make every chart tell the truth, every dashboard answer its question at a glance, the number that matters win the eye, and the palette stay readable for everyone.
playbooks.name: Make Data Legible
playbooks.tagline: Make every chart and dashboard tell the truth at a glance.

playbooks.desc: The interaction-and-UI sweep: navigation, menus, every interactive state, the empty screens, and the icon language — then the Fixer turns the findings into verified commits.
playbooks.name: Total UI Overhaul
playbooks.tagline: Overhaul the whole UI layer — then ship the fixes as commits.

playbooks.desc: One niche, end to end: map it, prove the pain, tear down the field, size it, position the entry, stress the model, then rule.
playbooks.name: Founder Funnel
playbooks.tagline: Pressure-test a startup idea before you write a line.

playbooks.desc: Seventy-two hours of truth for one idea: is the pain real, who already fights for the money, go or no.
playbooks.name: Gut Check
playbooks.tagline: 72 hours of truth for one idea: go or no.

playbooks.desc: Reach every user: usable in any language, accessible to everyone, and findable by search and shared links.
playbooks.name: Ship to the World
playbooks.tagline: Make it usable, accessible, and findable everywhere.

playbooks.desc: Before the repo goes public: safe to expose, dependencies sound, and every license accounted for.
playbooks.name: OSS Release-Ready
playbooks.tagline: Everything to check before a repo goes public.

playbooks.desc: Optimize the whole funnel for a first-time visitor: audit every conversion surface, then cut friction, sharpen the visual hierarchy, tighten the copy, and make it findable — and the Fixer ships the highest-leverage lifts as commits.
playbooks.name: eCommerce Optimization
playbooks.tagline: Audit every conversion surface for a newcomer, then ship the biggest lifts as commits.

playbooks.desc: Get it, want it, keep it — walk every surface a visitor meets and optimize the whole experience: does a newcomer understand it, are the benefits shown not told, do the conversion surfaces and their proof earn the yes, does the first session land a win, and does anything bring them back — then the Fixer ships the biggest lifts as commits.
playbooks.name: Experience Optimization
playbooks.tagline: Comprehension to conversion to retention — audit every surface a visitor meets, then ship the biggest lifts as commits.

playbooks.desc: A limited, themed sprint for launch season — hunt the bugs, lock the doors, make it fast, rehearse the bad day, and get found. One themed bundle, five reports, ready to ship.
playbooks.name: Ship-It Week
playbooks.tagline: Launch season, in one themed bundle of five audits.

playbooks.desc: A worked example of a co-branded playbook: pair change-risk review with a full bug hunt, then turn the findings into verified commits. This is the template a partner tool or creator drops their brand into — the content and cut are shared.
playbooks.name: Deep Code Review
playbooks.tagline: Example co-branded review sequence — how partner playbooks look.

playbooks.desc: The security pass before you expose anything: secrets, access control, injection paths, and an attacker's-eye threat model — then the Fixer turns the findings into verified commits.
playbooks.name: Harden Before Ship
playbooks.tagline: Lock the doors before you ship — then ship the fixes as commits.

playbooks.desc: The runtime data layer, end to end: the queries that will not scale, the integrity the schema does not enforce, and whether you could actually recover from data loss.
playbooks.name: Data-Layer Tune-Up
playbooks.tagline: Make the data layer fast, sound, and recoverable.

playbooks.desc: Sharpen the developer inner loop: time the edit-to-feedback cycle, make failures easy to diagnose, and shorten the wait between a push and a trustworthy CI signal.
playbooks.name: DX Tune-Up
playbooks.tagline: Cut the friction out of every change your team makes.

playbooks.desc: Earn a green suite you can trust: find the coverage gaps, strengthen the tests that assert nothing, kill the flakes, and rebalance a top-heavy pyramid.
playbooks.name: Test Confidence
playbooks.tagline: Make green actually mean green.

playbooks.desc: Everything a developer meets when they build on you: webhooks, the SDK, versioning, rate limits, and the first-hour docs — then the Fixer ships the fixes as commits.
playbooks.name: Ship a Public API
playbooks.tagline: Make an API developers actually want to build on.

playbooks.desc: The responsibility pass for an AI product: fairness across users, grounded and honest outputs, prompt-injection resistance, transparency, and the provenance of the data behind the model.
playbooks.name: Responsible AI Review
playbooks.tagline: Is your AI fair, grounded, honest, and defensible?

playbooks.desc: Whether it survives a bad day: graceful degradation when a dependency dies, the failure experiments worth running, the first bottleneck under load, abuse defenses, and incident readiness.
playbooks.name: Production Resilience
playbooks.tagline: Rehearse the bad day before it happens.

playbooks.desc: The operational-privacy layer regulators actually check: honest consent, real data-subject rights, encryption and key management, and a trustworthy audit trail.
playbooks.name: Privacy & Compliance
playbooks.tagline: Respect the user — and be able to prove it.

playbooks.desc: A speed-only sweep: profile the hot paths, hold a latency budget, fix the queries that will not scale, cut the bundle the browser downloads, and mine the churny hotspots.
playbooks.name: Make It Fast
playbooks.tagline: Five audits, one goal — make it fast.

playbooks.desc: Taking over an unfamiliar or orphaned project: get onboarded, read its health, map the debt, and find where the knowledge is dangerously concentrated.
playbooks.name: Inherit a Codebase
playbooks.tagline: Everything to learn before you own someone else's code.

playbooks.desc: Change with a net: assess the risk of the change, shore up the tests around it, simplify, and let the Fixer land the cleanup as verified commits.
playbooks.name: Refactor Safely
playbooks.tagline: Refactor without holding your breath.

playbooks.desc: Take money off the bill: audit infrastructure spend, the token economics of any AI, dead weight to prune, and the latency budget that right-sizes resources.
playbooks.name: Cost Down
playbooks.tagline: Find the spend you can cut without anyone noticing.

playbooks.desc: For AI-agent teams: the token economics of the loop, whether the model tier fits the job, and the context engineering that stops you paying for tokens you do not need.
playbooks.name: Agent Cost Control
playbooks.tagline: Stop overpaying for every agent call.

playbooks.desc: A themed start-of-year sweep: read the vitals, prune the dead weight, simplify what remains, and synthesize next year's roadmap — one seasonal bundle.
playbooks.name: New-Year Reset
playbooks.tagline: Start the year on a clean, clear codebase.

playbooks.desc: A worked example of a sponsored playbook: a curated performance sequence a sponsor's brand wraps — logo, name, and voice around shared content, with the placement paid. Swap in a real sponsor to ship one.
playbooks.name: Sponsored Speed Bundle
playbooks.tagline: Example sponsored bundle — how a paid placement looks.
